# terrible_cisco_meraki_organizations_assurance_alerts_overview_info (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Active query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by active alerts defaults to true.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `category` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Category query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by category.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `deviceTags` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. DeviceTags query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by device tags.
- `deviceTypes` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. DeviceTypes query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by device types.
- `dismissed` (Boolean) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Dismissed query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by dismissed alerts defaults to false.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `headers` (String) Additional headers.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `networkId` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. NetworkId query parameter. Optional parameter to filter alerts overview by network ids.
- `organizationId` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. OrganizationId path parameter. Organization ID.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `resolved` (Boolean) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Resolved query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by resolved alerts defaults to false.
- `serials` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Serials query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by primary device serial.
- `severity` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Severity query parameter. Optional parameter to filter alerts overview by severity type.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `suppressAlertsForOfflineNodes` (Boolean) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. SuppressAlertsForOfflineNodes query parameter. When set to true the api will only return connectivity alerts for a given device if that device is in an offline state. This only applies to devices. This is ignored when resolved is true. Example If a Switch has a VLan Mismatch and is Unreachable. Only the Unreachable alert will be returned. Defaults to false.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `tsEnd` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. TsEnd query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by end timestamp.
- `tsStart` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. TsStart query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by starting timestamp.
- `types` (String) Information module for Organizations Assurance Alerts Overview Info. Types query parameter. Optional parameter to filter by alert type.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `meraki_response` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco Meraki Python SDK
